attributes.name: AT

signatures.description: Sets position and size. All parameters are optional and can be omitted (e.g., AT(,,width,height)). Runtime library provides defaults for omitted parameters.

attributes.description: Defines the position and size of the structure or control. The x,y position is relative and dependent upon the statement on which the AT attribute is placed. Values are measured in dialog units for WINDOW/APPLICATION (one-quarter average character width by one-eighth average character height). All parameters are optional.

attributes.name: USE

signatures.description: Variable, field number, and equate label

attributes.description: Specifies a field equate label for the control or structure, or a variable for the control to update. With a variable parameter, supplies the control with a variable to update by operator entry (on window) or provide value to print (on report). All parameters after the first are optional.

attributes.name: COLOR

signatures.description: Specifies display or print colors. Color parameter is required, selectedFore and selectedBack are optional.

attributes.description: Specifies display or print color. The color parameter specifies background color (PROP:Background). The selectedFore and selectedBack parameters specify colors for selected text on controls that can receive focus. The last two parameters are optional and not valid in REPORT.

attributes.name: FONT

signatures.description: Specifies the default display font. All parameters are optional and can be omitted (e.g., FONT(,size) or FONT('Arial',12,,FONT:bold)). Runtime library provides defaults for omitted parameters.

attributes.description: Specifies the default display font. Typeface is font name, size in points, color as RGB LONG value, style for weight/italic/underline/strikeout, and charset for international character sets. All parameters are optional.

attributes.name: HIDE
attributes.description: Specifies the control does not appear when the WINDOW or APPLICATION is first opened. UNHIDE must be used to display it. In a REPORT, the control does not print unless UNHIDE is used.

attributes.name: DISABLE
attributes.description: Specifies the control is dimmed (grayed out) when the window is first opened. The control cannot receive focus and does not respond to user input. ENABLE must be used to activate it.

attributes.name: CENTER
attributes.description: Sets the window to be centered on screen when first opened. The window is positioned at the center of the screen or MDI parent window client area.

attributes.name: MODAL
attributes.description: Sets the window as system modal. A modal window must be closed before the user can interact with any other window in the application or system.

attributes.name: MDI
attributes.description: Sets the window as an MDI (Multiple Document Interface) child window. MDI child windows are contained within the client area of an APPLICATION structure.

attributes.name: SYSTEM
attributes.description: Adds a system menu to the window or application. The system menu appears as an icon in the upper left corner and provides minimize, maximize, restore, and close options.

attributes.name: MAX
attributes.description: Adds a maximize button to the window or application title bar, allowing the user to maximize the window to full screen.

attributes.name: ICON
attributes.description: Sets the icon for the window, application, or button. The filename parameter specifies the .ICO file to use.

attributes.name: STATUS
attributes.description: Adds a status bar to the bottom of the window or application. The height parameter specifies the height in dialog units. The align parameter specifies text alignment.

attributes.name: IMM
attributes.description: Sets immediate event notification for the control. Changes to the control generate an EVENT:Accepted immediately rather than waiting for the user to press Enter or Tab.

attributes.name: READONLY
attributes.description: Sets the control as display-only. The control can receive focus and be tabbed to, but the user cannot edit its contents.

attributes.name: REQ
attributes.description: Sets required entry for the control. The user must enter a value in the control before the window can be closed or focus moved to certain other controls.

attributes.name: AUTO
attributes.description:
Two distinct uses:

**CONTROL attribute**: Sets the USE variable to automatically re-display when changed by program code. Normally, changes to the USE variable are only displayed when the window is opened or the field is updated.

**Variable declaration attribute**: Specifies the variable has no initial value and is not initialized. Without AUTO, numeric variables are initialized to zero and strings to blanks. AUTO variables contain whatever value was in memory at allocation time.

attributes.name: SKIP
attributes.description: Sets the control to be skipped when the user presses Tab. The control cannot receive focus through Tab key navigation, but can still receive focus through mouse clicks or SELECT statement.

attributes.name: GRAY
attributes.description: Sets 3-D look background using the Windows button face color. Provides a raised 3-D appearance for the control or window background.

attributes.name: FROM
attributes.description: Sets the data source for a LIST or COMBO control. The queue parameter specifies the QUEUE structure containing the data to display.

attributes.name: FORMAT
attributes.description: Sets the layout for LIST or COMBO control. The format string specifies column widths, headers, and alignment for multi-column lists.

attributes.name: RANGE
attributes.description: Sets range limits for entry control. Validates that entered values fall within the specified low to high range. Generates EVENT:RangeHigh or EVENT:RangeLow if violated.

attributes.name: VALUE
attributes.description: Sets the value assigned to the USE variable when a RADIO button or CHECK box is selected. Allows multiple radio buttons or check boxes to set different values to the same variable.

attributes.name: HSCROLL
attributes.description: Adds a horizontal scroll bar to the window or control.

attributes.name: VSCROLL
attributes.description: Adds a vertical scroll bar to the window or control.

attributes.name: HVSCROLL
attributes.description: Adds both horizontal and vertical scroll bars to the window or control.

attributes.name: RESIZE
attributes.description: Sets the window border to allow resizing. The user can drag the window edges to change the window size.

attributes.name: DOUBLE
attributes.description: Sets a double-line window border. Cannot be combined with RESIZE.

attributes.name: NOFRAME
attributes.description: Sets the window to have no border frame. The window appears without a title bar or border.

attributes.name: TIP
attributes.description: Sets 'balloon help' text that appears when the mouse hovers over the control. The text parameter specifies the tooltip message.

attributes.name: KEY
attributes.description: Sets the execution keycode for the control. When the specified key is pressed, the control is selected and executes.

attributes.name: DEFAULT
attributes.description: Sets the button as the default button for the window. When the user presses Enter, this button executes unless focus is on another button.

attributes.name: MSG
attributes.description: Sets the message to display in the status bar when the control has focus or menu item is highlighted.

attributes.name: HLP
attributes.description: Sets the on-line help identifier for context-sensitive help. The helpID parameter specifies the help topic to display.

attributes.name: ALRT
attributes.description: Sets window 'hot' keys. Defines keys that generate an alert when pressed in the window.

attributes.name: FILL
attributes.description: Sets the fill color for BOX, ELLIPSE, or REGION controls. The color parameter specifies the interior fill color.

attributes.name: TIMER
attributes.description: Sets periodic event generation. The interval parameter specifies time in hundredths of a second between EVENT:Timer events.

attributes.name: BEVEL
attributes.description: Sets 3-D effect border for the control. The style parameter defines the bevel appearance (raised, inset, etc.).

attributes.name: BOXED
attributes.description: Sets a border around a GROUP of controls. Draws a rectangular border encompassing all controls in the group.

attributes.name: CAP
attributes.description: Sets text entry to all capitals. Automatically converts all entered text to uppercase.

attributes.name: UPR
attributes.description: Sets text entry to all uppercase. Similar to CAP, converts entered text to uppercase.

attributes.name: CENTERED
attributes.description: Centers an image within its control area. The image is displayed centered rather than at top-left.

attributes.name: TILED
attributes.description: Tiles an image to fill the control area. The image is repeated horizontally and vertically.

attributes.name: WALLPAPER
attributes.description: Sets a background image for the window. The filename parameter specifies the image file to use as wallpaper.

attributes.name: CHECK
attributes.description: Creates an on/off menu item with a checkmark. Used in MENUs to show item selection state.

attributes.name: RADIO
attributes.description: Creates a radio button menu item. Only one radio item in a group can be selected at a time.

attributes.name: SEPARATOR
attributes.description: Creates a separator line in a menu. Draws a horizontal line to visually separate menu items.

attributes.name: FLAT
attributes.description: Sets buttons to have a flat appearance. Buttons appear flat until the mouse hovers over them.

attributes.name: MARK
attributes.description: Enables multiple selection mode for LIST controls. Allows the user to select multiple items.

attributes.name: DROP
attributes.description: Sets list box behavior for COMBO or LIST controls. Creates a drop-down list.

attributes.name: COLUMN
attributes.description: Sets list box to use column-based highlighting. The highlight bar extends across all columns.

attributes.name: GRID
attributes.description: Sets grid line display and color for LIST controls. Shows lines between rows and columns.

attributes.name: NOBAR
attributes.description: Removes the highlight bar from LIST controls. Items can be selected but no visual highlight appears.

attributes.name: MASK
attributes.description: Sets pattern editing for data entry. The pattern parameter defines input format mask.

attributes.name: PASSWORD
attributes.description: Hides data entry by displaying asterisks. Used for password entry fields.

attributes.name: SINGLE
attributes.description: Sets TEXT control for single line entry. Limits text entry to one line only.

attributes.name: SCROLL
attributes.description: Enables scrolling for TEXT control. Allows vertical scrolling through text content.

attributes.name: MAXIMIZE
attributes.description: Opens the window in maximized state. Window fills the entire screen or MDI parent area.

attributes.name: ICONIZE
attributes.description: Opens the window as an icon (minimized). Window appears minimized on first open.

attributes.name: FULL
attributes.description: Sets window to full-screen mode. Window occupies the entire display with no borders.

attributes.name: TOOLBOX
attributes.description: Sets window as a toolbox. Creates a floating tool palette window.

attributes.name: DOCK
attributes.description: Enables window docking capability. Window can be docked to edges of parent window.

attributes.name: DOCKED
attributes.description: Opens dockable window in docked state. Window appears docked at open rather than floating.

attributes.name: DIM

signatures.description: Dimensions the variable as an array. Up to 3 dimensions supported.

attributes.description: Declares the variable as an array. The DIM attribute specifies the number of elements in each dimension (up to 3 dimensions). Array elements are accessed using subscripts: variable[index] or variable[row,col].

attributes.name: OVER

signatures.description: Specifies the variable label to overlay in memory

attributes.description: Specifies that this variable occupies the same memory location as another variable. Both variables share the same memory, allowing different interpretations of the same data. Useful for type conversion or viewing data in different formats.

attributes.name: NAME

signatures.description: Specifies the external name for the field

attributes.description: Specifies an alternate 'external' name for the field. Used primarily in FILE declarations to map internal field names to external file field names. The external name is used when reading/writing to the file.

attributes.name: EXTERNAL
attributes.description: Specifies that the variable or procedure is defined in an external library (not in the current program). The variable's memory is allocated externally, not by the Clarion program. Cannot be used within FILE, QUEUE, or GROUP declarations.

attributes.name: DLL

signatures.description: Declares a procedure, variable, FILE, QUEUE, GROUP, or CLASS defined externally in a .DLL. The optional flag is a numeric constant, equate, or any project-level conditional compilation symbol â€” when non-zero the attribute is active; zero or omitted means inactive.

attributes.description: Marks a declaration as residing in an external .DLL. Required for 32-bit apps because .DLLs are relocatable in a flat address space, requiring an extra compiler dereference. Variable/FILE/QUEUE/GROUP/CLASS declarations must also have the EXTERNAL attribute. The flag is a numeric constant or any conditional compilation symbol defined via #pragma define(ident=>value) in the project file or the IDE's Conditional Compilation Symbols dialog. The ABC template chain uses the pre-defined _ABCDllMode_ symbol (set automatically by the project system's dll_mode pragma) as a common example.

attributes.name: STATIC
attributes.description: Specifies static memory allocation for procedure-local variables. Static variables retain their values between procedure calls (not reinitialized). For QUEUE/GROUP in procedures, allocates fixed memory instead of stack allocation.

attributes.name: THREAD
attributes.description: Declares thread-local storage. Each execution thread gets its own separate copy of the variable. Ensures thread-safe access in multi-threaded applications. Also implicitly adds STATIC attribute for procedure-local data.

attributes.name: PRIVATE
attributes.description: Specifies that the variable, method, or property is not visible outside the module containing the CLASS methods. Only valid within a CLASS declaration. Provides encapsulation and information hiding.

attributes.name: PROTECTED
attributes.description: Specifies that the variable, method, or property is not visible outside the base CLASS and derived CLASS methods. Only valid within a CLASS declaration. Allows inheritance while preventing external access.

attributes.name: TYPE
attributes.description: Declares that this structure can be used as a data type for other variable declarations. Allows GROUP/QUEUE/CLASS to be reused as a template. Variables can then be declared using this type, inheriting all its fields and structure.

attributes.name: PRE

signatures.description: Specifies the prefix for field labels

attributes.description: Declares a field label prefix for the structure. All field labels within the structure are automatically prefixed with this value. Used for namespacing and avoiding label conflicts. Example: PRE(CUS) makes field 'Name' accessible as 'CUS:Name'.

attributes.name: BINDABLE
attributes.description: Allows dynamic expression evaluation on the structure using BIND() and EVALUATE(). Makes field names available at runtime for dynamic queries and calculations. Fields must have NAME attribute for BINDABLE to work.

attributes.name: DRIVER
attributes.description: Specifies the file driver type for the FILE declaration. Common drivers: 'TOPSPEED' (TPS), 'ASCII', 'DOS', 'BASIC', 'CLARION', 'BTRIEVE', 'MSSQL', 'ODBC'. Required attribute for FILE declarations.

attributes.name: CREATE
attributes.description: Specifies that the file should be created if it doesn't exist when opened. Without CREATE, attempting to open a non-existent file will result in an error. Typically used with CREATE mode file operations.

attributes.name: OWNER

signatures.description: Specifies the owner password for encrypted files

attributes.description: Specifies the owner password for encrypted TopSpeed files. Required when using ENCRYPT attribute. The owner has full access including ability to change encryption and structure. Used with TopSpeed driver only.

attributes.name: ENCRYPT
attributes.description: Specifies that the file should be encrypted. Requires OWNER attribute to specify the encryption password. Provides data security by encrypting file contents. Used with TopSpeed driver only.

attributes.name: RECLAIM
attributes.description: Specifies that deleted record space should be reclaimed and reused. Without RECLAIM, deleted records leave gaps in the file. RECLAIM compacts the file by reusing deleted space, reducing file size over time.

attributes.name: IMPLEMENTS
attributes.description: Adds additional methods to a CLASS by implementing an INTERFACE. When a class implements an interface, it inherits all methods defined in the INTERFACE and must provide implementations for them. A class may implement multiple interfaces.

attributes.name: LINK

signatures.description: Names a file to add to the link list

attributes.description: Names a file to add to the link list for the current project when using this CLASS. linkfile is a string constant; .OBJ extension is assumed if none is given. Normally the same as the MODULE parameter but may explicitly name a .LIB or .OBJ file. The flag is a numeric constant or any conditional compilation symbol defined via #pragma define(ident=>value) in the project file or the IDE's Conditional Compilation Symbols dialog â€” when non-zero the file is linked, zero means inactive. The ABC template chain uses _ABCLinkMode_ as a common example. LINK is only valid on a CLASS structure.

attributes.name: MODULE

signatures.description: Specifies the source file containing CLASS method implementations

attributes.description: CLASS attribute that specifies the source code file containing the CLASS method implementations. The module file must be included in the project. Used to separate class definition from implementation. Note: MODULE is also a keyword used in MAP blocks to declare procedure prototypes - see builtins.

attributes.name: RAW
attributes.description: PROCEDURE attribute. Specifies that STRING or GROUP parameters pass only the memory address without passing the length. Also alters behavior of ? and *? parameters. Used in MAP or CLASS procedure prototypes for 3GL language compatibility.

attributes.name: PASCAL
attributes.description: PROCEDURE attribute. Specifies PASCAL calling convention (left to right stack-based parameter passing). Windows-standard calling convention for 32-bit applications. Used in MAP or CLASS procedure prototypes for compatibility with third-party libraries and Windows API.

attributes.name: C
attributes.description: PROCEDURE attribute. Specifies C calling convention (right to left stack-based parameter passing). Used in MAP or CLASS procedure prototypes for compatibility with third-party C libraries not compiled with TopSpeed compiler.

attributes.name: PROC
attributes.description: PROCEDURE attribute. Specifies that a PROCEDURE with a return type may be called without using the return value, without generating a compiler warning. Can be used in MAP or CLASS procedure prototypes.

attributes.name: VIRTUAL
attributes.description: CLASS method attribute. Specifies the PROCEDURE is a virtual method of a CLASS structure. Virtual methods can be overridden in derived classes using late binding (polymorphism).

attributes.name: REPLACE
attributes.description: CLASS method attribute. Specifies that the 'Construct' or 'Destruct' PROCEDURE in the derived CLASS completely replaces the constructor or destructor of its parent CLASS (does not call parent constructor/destructor).

attributes.name: DERIVED
attributes.description: CLASS method attribute. Specifies the PROCEDURE is a derived method of a CLASS structure. There must be a matching prototype in the parent class that this method overrides.

attributes.name: ONCE
attributes.description: INCLUDE attribute. Prevents data contained in the INCLUDE file from being compiled more than once. Eliminates duplicate definition compile warnings or errors when the same include file is referenced multiple times.

attributes.name: ABSOLUTE
attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Ensures the DETAIL, group HEADER, or FOOTER structure always prints at a fixed position on the page. The AT position is relative to the top-left corner of the page rather than following normal flow.

attributes.name: ALONE
attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Specifies the DETAIL, group HEADER, or FOOTER prints on the page without any FORM, or page HEADER or FOOTER. Commonly used for report title pages and grand total pages.

attributes.name: ANGLE

signatures.description: Rotation in tenths of degrees, counter-clockwise from horizontal. Valid range: -3600 to 3600.

attributes.description: Sets the display or print angle of a STRING control. The FONT must be a TrueType font. Useful for vertical or rotated labels in windows and reports.

attributes.name: AUTOSIZE
attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Specifies the OLE object automatically resizes itself when the OLE container control's AT attribute parameters change at runtime.

attributes.name: AVE

signatures.description: Optional numeric variable to receive intermediate average values.

attributes.description: Report total attribute. Calculates the average (arithmetic mean) of the STRING control's USE variable as DETAIL structures print. Reset with RESET or PAGE attributes. Typically placed in a group or page FOOTER.

attributes.name: BINARY
attributes.description: Specifies the MEMO or BLOB contains binary data (e.g. graphic images) rather than plain text. OEM conversion is not applied. Some file drivers assume binary fields are zero-padded rather than space-padded.

attributes.name: CLIP
attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Specifies the OLE object only displays what fits within the container control's AT size â€” larger objects are clipped to the top-left corner.

attributes.name: CNT

signatures.description: Optional numeric variable to receive intermediate count values.

attributes.description: Report total attribute. Counts the number of times DETAIL structures have been printed. Reset with RESET or PAGE. Typically placed in a group or page FOOTER.

attributes.name: COMPATIBILITY

signatures.description: Integer compatibility mode constant (usually 0; some objects require 1).

attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Sets a compatibility mode for OLE or OCX objects that require special handling. Normally zero; some objects (e.g. Windows bitmap editor) require one.

attributes.name: CURSOR

signatures.description: Name of a .CUR file or a CURSOR: equate from EQUATES.CLW.

attributes.description: Specifies the mouse cursor displayed when the pointer is over the structure or control. Inherited by contained controls unless overridden. Standard cursors are defined as CURSOR:Arrow, CURSOR:Wait, CURSOR:IBeam, etc. in EQUATES.CLW.

attributes.name: DELAY

signatures.description: Delay in hundredths of a second between first and second event generation.

attributes.description: Specifies the delay between first and second event generation for auto-repeating controls. For a BUTTON with IMM, this is the delay between first and second EVENT:Accepted. For a SPIN, the delay between first and second EVENT:NewSelection. Assigning 0 to PROP:DELAY resets the default.

attributes.name: DOCUMENT

signatures.description: Fully-qualified path of the OLE server data file to open.

attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Creates an OLE object from a data file specific to an OLE server application (e.g. an Excel spreadsheet). The filename must be a fully-qualified path unless the file is in the same directory as the application.

attributes.name: DRAGID

signatures.description: One or more string identifiers (up to 16) used to match against DROPID targets. Signatures starting with ~ allow cross-application drag.

attributes.description: Specifies a LIST or REGION control as a drag-and-drop host. The signature strings match against DROPID attributes on target controls. A successful drag-drop requires at least one identical signature in both DRAGID and DROPID. Drag signatures cannot start with '!'.

attributes.name: DROPID

signatures.description: One or more string identifiers (up to 16). '~TEXT' accepts text from external apps; '~FILE' accepts filenames from Windows Explorer.

attributes.description: Specifies a control as a drag-and-drop target. Works with DRAGID â€” at least one signature must match between host and target. '~TEXT' and '~FILE' are predefined signatures for external drag-drop from other applications.

attributes.name: DUP
attributes.description: Allows multiple records with the same key values in a FILE. Without DUP, attempting to ADD or PUT records with duplicate key values generates a 'Creates Duplicate Key' error. INDEX declarations always allow duplicates (DUP is unnecessary on INDEX).

attributes.name: FILTER

signatures.description: A string constant containing a logical Clarion expression. All variables used must be BINDed.

attributes.description: Specifies a filter expression to evaluate records included in the VIEW. The expression may reference any field in the VIEW at all JOIN levels. All variables in the expression must be BINDed. SQL databases can convert MATCH() calls to SQL WHERE clauses for server-side filtering.

attributes.name: FIRST
attributes.description: Positions a MENU or ITEM at the beginning when a window's MENUBAR is merged into the global pulldown menu. Global FIRST items have higher priority than local FIRST items in the merge order.

attributes.name: LAST
attributes.description: Positions a MENU or ITEM at the end when a window's MENUBAR is merged into the global pulldown menu. Items without FIRST or LAST appear between the two groups in the merge order.

attributes.name: INNER
attributes.description: Specifies the JOIN declares an inner join instead of the default left outer join. With INNER, only primary file records that have matching secondary file records are retrieved. Inner joins are generally more efficient than outer joins.

attributes.name: INS
attributes.description: Sets insert typing mode for an ENTRY control (new characters push existing characters right). Contrast with OVR (overwrite mode). Not valid for TEXT controls.

attributes.name: OVR
attributes.description: Sets overwrite typing mode for an ENTRY control (new characters replace existing characters). Contrast with INS (insert mode). Not valid for TEXT controls.

attributes.name: JOIN
attributes.description: Specifies the SHEET's TABs display all on one row instead of wrapping to multiple rows. Left and right scroll buttons appear together at the right end to allow scrolling through all TABs.

attributes.name: LANDSCAPE
attributes.description: Sets the default page orientation for the REPORT to landscape mode. If omitted, printing defaults to portrait mode.

attributes.name: LINEWIDTH

signatures.description: Positive integer specifying thickness in pixels (WINDOW) or report coordinate units (REPORT).

attributes.description: Specifies the thickness of a LINE control or the border of BOX and ELLIPSE controls. In WINDOW structures the unit is pixels; in REPORT structures the unit depends on the report measurement (THOUS, MM, POINTS, or dialog units).

attributes.name: MIN

signatures.description: Optional numeric variable to receive intermediate minimum values.

attributes.description: Report total attribute. Calculates the minimum value the STRING control's USE variable has held so far. Reset with RESET or PAGE. Typically placed in a group or page FOOTER.

attributes.name: NOCASE
attributes.description: Makes the KEY or INDEX sort order case-insensitive. All alphabetic characters in key fields are converted to uppercase as entries are written. Case conversion does not affect the actual stored data.

attributes.name: NOMERGE
attributes.description: Prevents the MENUBAR or TOOLBAR from merging with the global menu or toolbar. On a WINDOW, the window's own menu/toolbar overwrites the global menu/toolbar when that window has focus. On APPLICATION, indicates the menu/toolbar is local and shows only when no MDI child windows are open.

attributes.name: NOSHEET
attributes.description: Specifies the SHEET's TABs display without a visible sheet container around the controls. Creates a 'floating tab' appearance.

attributes.name: OEM
attributes.description: Specifies the FILE contains non-English string data stored in the OEM ASCII character set. String data is automatically translated between ANSI (for Windows display) and OEM ASCII (for disk storage). Not supported by all file drivers.

attributes.name: OPEN

signatures.description: OLE Compound Storage file and object name in the form 'Filename\!ObjectName'.

attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Opens a previously saved OLE object from an OLE Compound Storage file. Container properties saved with the object are reloaded automatically.

attributes.name: OPT
attributes.description: Excludes records from the KEY or INDEX when all component fields contain null values (zero for numeric, all spaces for strings). Reduces key size by omitting blank/zero entries.

attributes.name: ORDER

signatures.description: Comma-separated expression list. Prefix with '-' for descending order. All variables must be BINDed.

attributes.description: Specifies the sort order expression list for records in the VIEW. Expressions are evaluated left-to-right (leftmost = most significant). A leading '-' on an expression sorts descending. For SQL views, PROP:SQLOrder is an SQL-only equivalent.

attributes.name: PAGE
attributes.description: Report total attribute. Specifies that the CNT, SUM, AVE, MIN, or MAX total is reset to zero when a page break occurs.

attributes.name: PAGEAFTER

signatures.description: Optional page number to begin on the next page. Use -1 to auto-increment page number.

attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Initiates page overflow after the structure prints. The structure prints first, then the page FOOTER, followed by the FORM and page HEADER on the new page. The optional newpage parameter resets the automatic page number.

attributes.name: PAGEBEFORE

signatures.description: Optional page number to begin on the new page. Use -1 to auto-increment page number.

attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Initiates page overflow before the structure prints. The page FOOTER and FORM/page HEADER print first, then the structure prints on the new page. The optional newpage parameter resets the automatic page number.

attributes.name: PAGENO

signatures.description: Optional numeric variable to receive the current page number.

attributes.description: Report attribute. Specifies the STRING control prints the current page number. Page numbers can be affected by PAGEBEFORE and PAGEAFTER attributes.

attributes.name: PALETTE

signatures.description: Number of hardware palette colors to reserve for this window.

attributes.description: Specifies how many hardware palette colors this window uses when it is the foreground window. Only applicable in 256-color mode. Values above 256 are not recommended. PROP:PALETTE returns the number of colors actually assigned (may be less than requested due to system reserves).

attributes.name: PAPER

signatures.description: type is a Windows paper size EQUATE (from EQUATES.CLW). width/height are only required when type is PAPER:User. Units default to 1/1000 inch regardless of THOUS/MM/POINTS.

attributes.description: Defines the paper size for a REPORT. Paper size equates (PAPER:Letter, PAPER:A4, PAPER:User, etc.) are in EQUATES.CLW. Width and height in thousandths of an inch are required only for PAPER:User. Not all printers support all paper sizes.

attributes.name: POINTS
attributes.description: Specifies report or window runtime property coordinates are measured in points (72 points per inch, both vertically and horizontally). One of three metric alternatives to dialog units; see also THOUS and MM.

attributes.name: PREVIEW

signatures.description: Label of a QUEUE or QUEUE field to receive the temporary metafile filenames (up to 64 chars each).

attributes.description: Sends report output to Windows metafiles (one per page) instead of directly to a printer. The queue receives the temporary metafile filenames for display in an IMAGE control. Temporary files are deleted when the REPORT is CLOSEd (remove from queue to preserve). Set PROP:FlushPreview to print.

attributes.name: PRIMARY
attributes.description: Designates the KEY as the relational primary key per Codd's definition: unique, includes all records, and does not allow null values in any component field. Implies no duplicates and no null entries.

attributes.name: RESET

signatures.description: Label of a BREAK structure at which the total resets to zero.

attributes.description: Report total attribute. Resets a CNT, SUM, AVE, MIN, or MAX total to zero at the specified BREAK group boundary. PROP:RESET returns 0 if not present, otherwise the break nesting depth.

attributes.name: ROUND
attributes.description: Specifies a BOX control displays with rounded corners. Valid in both Clarion Win32 windows and REPORT structures.

attributes.name: SPREAD
attributes.description: Specifies the TAB controls within a SHEET are evenly spaced across the full width of the SHEET.

attributes.name: STD

signatures.description: Integer constant or EQUATE from EQUATES.CLW (e.g. STD:Cut, STD:Copy, STD:Paste, STD:Undo, STD:WindowList).

attributes.description: Specifies the control activates a standard Windows action. The action is automatically executed by the runtime library and does not generate any events. Standard action equates (STD:Cut, STD:Copy, STD:Paste, STD:Undo, STD:WindowList, etc.) are in EQUATES.CLW.

attributes.name: STEP

signatures.description: Amount to increment or decrement the SPIN value within its RANGE.

attributes.description: Specifies the increment/decrement amount for a SPIN control within its RANGE. The default STEP is 1.0. Use decimal values for fractional steps (e.g. STEP(.05) for a 0.05 increment).

attributes.name: STRETCH
attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Specifies the OLE object stretches to completely fill the AT size of the container control. Does not preserve the object's aspect ratio (see ZOOM for aspect-ratio-preserving stretch).

attributes.name: SUM

signatures.description: Optional numeric variable to receive intermediate sum values.

attributes.description: Report total attribute. Calculates the running sum of the STRING control's USE variable as DETAIL structures print. Reset with RESET or PAGE. Typically placed in a group or page FOOTER.

attributes.name: TALLY

signatures.description: Comma-delimited list of DETAIL and/or BREAK structure labels on which to calculate the total.

attributes.description: Report total attribute. Specifies when to calculate an AVE, CNT, MAX, MIN, or SUM. The total is calculated each time any named DETAIL structure prints, or when a named BREAK boundary occurs. Used with the other report total attributes.

attributes.name: THOUS
attributes.description: Specifies report or window runtime property coordinates are measured in thousandths of an inch. One of three metric alternatives to dialog units; see also MM and POINTS.

attributes.name: MM
attributes.description: Specifies report or window runtime property coordinates are measured in millimeters. One of three metric alternatives to dialog units; see also THOUS and POINTS.

attributes.name: TOGETHER
attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Specifies the structure must print entirely on a single page rather than being split across a page boundary. Commonly used for multi-line structures such as mailing labels.

attributes.name: TRN
attributes.description: Specifies the control displays or prints transparently â€” only the pixels needed for each character are drawn, without obliterating the background. Useful for placing text over an IMAGE control. Valid in both Clarion Win32 windows and REPORT structures.

attributes.name: UP
attributes.description: Specifies the TAB control text appears vertically, reading upwards. Used with SHEET controls. If both UP and DOWN are present the text appears inverted (PROP:UpsideDown).

attributes.name: DOWN
attributes.description: Specifies the TAB control text appears vertically, reading downwards. Used with SHEET controls. If both UP and DOWN are present the text appears inverted (PROP:UpsideDown).

attributes.name: VCR

signatures.description: Optional field equate label of an ENTRY control to use as a locator when the user presses the '?' VCR button.

attributes.description: Adds VCR-style navigation buttons to a LIST or COMBO control: |< Top, << PageUp, < Up, ? Locate, > Down, >> PageDown, >| Bottom. The optional field parameter activates the locate button with the named ENTRY control. Setting PROP:VCR = TRUE adds buttons without the locate button.

attributes.name: WITHNEXT

signatures.description: Number of immediately following print structures that must print on the same page. Default is 1.

attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Ensures the structure always prints on the same page as the specified number of immediately following print structures from the same or nested BREAK. Eliminates 'widow' structures (group headers stranded at the bottom of a page).

attributes.name: WITHPRIOR

signatures.description: Number of immediately preceding print structures that must print on the same page. Default is 1.

attributes.description: Report structure attribute. Ensures the structure always prints on the same page as the specified number of immediately preceding print structures from the same or nested BREAK. Eliminates 'orphan' structures (group footers stranded at the top of a new page).

attributes.name: WIZARD
attributes.description: Specifies a SHEET control that does not display its TAB controls. Allows the program to direct the user through each TAB in a specified sequence, typically with 'Next' and 'Previous' buttons â€” creating a wizard-style interface.

attributes.name: ZOOM
attributes.description: OLE control attribute (write-only). Specifies the OLE object stretches to fill the AT size of the container control while maintaining the object's aspect ratio (contrast with STRETCH which ignores aspect ratio).

attributes.name: ABOVE
attributes.description: Specifies that the TAB controls appear at the top of the SHEET (the default position). The optional width parameter sets the size of the TAB controls in dialog units.

signatures.description: Optional width of TAB controls in dialog units (PROP:AboveSize).

attributes.name: BELOW
attributes.description: Specifies that the TAB controls appear at the bottom of the SHEET. The optional width parameter sets the size of the TAB controls in dialog units.

signatures.description: Optional width of TAB controls in dialog units (PROP:BelowSize).

attributes.name: EXTEND
attributes.description: Specifies attributes for a particular document output type (HTML, XML, TXT, PDF, ALL). A multi-purpose storage tag for information embedded in the generated report WMF file, later parsed and passed to appropriate processing code.

signatures.description: Attribute string(s), optionally prefixed with a document type: HTML(...), XML(...), TXT(...), PDF(...), ALL(...).

attributes.name: LAYOUT
attributes.description: Sets the orientation of window controls and field sequence. Style 1 mirrors the layout (right-to-left). Style 0 maintains left-to-right. Setting LAYOUT on APPLICATION cascades to child windows.

attributes.name: REPEAT
attributes.description: Specifies the rate of event generation for automatically repeating controls. For a BUTTON with IMM, this is the EVENT:Accepted rate. For a SPIN, this is EVENT:NewSelection rate. Assigning 0 resets to the default.

signatures.description: Rate in hundredths of a second.

attributes.name: SMOOTH
attributes.description: Activates a smooth incremental display of the PROGRESS control instead of the standard block format.

signatures.description: No parameters; presence activates smooth display.

attributes.name: VERTICAL
attributes.description: Allows the PROGRESS control to operate from bottom to top instead of the default left-to-right. Resize the control to a vertical orientation accordingly.

signatures.description: No parameters; presence enables vertical mode.
